summary: Re-tested AI matchmaking fix end-to-end. All 15 backend pytest tests pass (auth, profile, upload, matches GET/POST with non-empty assertions, file auth check 401/403/200, cookie security Secure+HttpOnly+SameSite=none, achievements, logout). Frontend E2E verified: register -> onboarding (Python, Design) -> dashboard renders 4 real AI match cards (e.g., 'Alex Garcia', collaboration idea 'Pair program a simple app...'), clicking a card navigates to /match/0, conversation starters render with real content ('How did you start learning Python?'), and copy click fires the 'Copied to clipboard!' toast. The CRITICAL AI matchmaking bug is FIXED. Two minor issues remain: (1) MatchDetails copyToClipboard does not catch clipboard write promise rejection -> CRA error overlay appears when clipboard permission is denied; (2) /api/auth/refresh still uses secure=False samesite=lax inconsistently.

minor.issue: server.py line 323 sets access_token cookie with secure=False, samesite='lax', while /auth/login and /auth/register use secure=True, samesite='none'. Refreshed tokens may be rejected by browser on subsequent cross-site fetches in HTTPS preview.

The markdown-fence stripping regex at server.py line 149 is `re.sub(r'^```(?:json)?\\s*|\\s*```$', ...)`. Because of the doubled backslashes in the raw string, the regex literally matches `\s` (backslash+s) instead of whitespace. The fix only works because of the secondary fallback (extract substring between first '{' and last '}'). Simplify to: `cleaned = re.sub(r'^```(?:json)?\s*|\s*```$', '', response.strip(), flags=re.MULTILINE).strip()` (single backslashes) or rely solely on the brace-extract fallback.

ui_bugs.issue: navigator.clipboard.writeText() returns a Promise that may reject (e.g., permission denied in some browsers/headless contexts). It is invoked fire-and-forget, so the rejection surfaces as 'Uncaught (in promise) NotAllowedError: Write permission denied' and triggers the CRA dev error overlay, hiding the page. Wrap with .catch() and optionally fall back to document.execCommand('copy') or show a toast.error.

action_items: Wrap navigator.clipboard.writeText in try/catch (or .catch()) in MatchDetails.jsx so clipboard permission rejection does not raise an uncaught error overlay.
Make /api/auth/refresh use the same cookie attributes as login/register (secure=True, samesite='none').

Clean up the markdown-fence regex in generate_matches_with_ai to use single backslashes (the current double-backslash form does not match whitespace; works only by accident due to the brace-extract fallback).
critical_code_review_comments: MatchDetails.jsx copyToClipboard does not await/handle navigator.clipboard.writeText promise rejection -> dev overlay disrupts UX.
server.py line 323 (/auth/refresh) cookie security flags inconsistent with /auth/login and /auth/register.
server.py line 149 markdown-strip regex uses `\\s` in raw string, which is literal `\s` text not whitespace class. The defensive brace-extract fallback masks this bug. Refactor for clarity.
server.py is 536 lines, monolithic. Recommend splitting into routers (auth.py, profile.py, matches.py) and services (ai_service.py, storage_service.py).

server.py line 403 `if match_doc and match_doc.get('matches'):` — when AI fails and stores empty matches[], next GET will skip cache and re-trigger expensive LLM call (no cooldown/TTL). Add backoff or set a `last_attempted_at` to avoid hammering OpenAI.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added non-empty match assertions, file auth 401/403/owner, cookie Secure/HttpOnly/SameSite=none checks; 15 tests total)

success_rate.frontend: 95% (core flow + AI matches + copy toast all work; clipboard promise rejection overlay is the only frontend issue)

test_credentials: admin@skillpartner.com / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). E2E user created at runtime each run.
seed_data_creation: Admin user auto-seeded by server.py startup. E2E test users created dynamically (e.g., e2e_<8hex>@example.com).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All previous CRITICAL issues are resolved. AI matchmaking returns real GPT-4o data (verified via backend assert len(matches)>0 and frontend visible match cards). File download enforces ownership (401 unauth, 403 other user, 200 owner). Cookies on login/register are Secure+HttpOnly+SameSite=none. Only 3 minor follow-ups remain: (a) copyToClipboard promise rejection in MatchDetails.jsx, (b) /api/auth/refresh cookie flags inconsistency, (c) regex cleanup. Backend tests file is the source of regression at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py.
rca of the issue: Previous critical bug (AI matchmaking returning empty matches due to GPT-4o markdown-wrapped JSON) is RESOLVED. The current implementation in generate_matches_with_ai (a) updates the system prompt to demand raw JSON, (b) attempts to strip markdown fences via regex, and (c) extracts substring between first '{' and last '}' as a defensive fallback, then validates non-empty matches before returning. Confirmed via curl + pytest + Playwright that GET /api/matches returns 4 matches with all required fields (partner_name, complementary_skills, compatibility, collaboration_idea, conversation_starters).
